stimulus: Award-winning composer Tania Vasquez has worked for decades as both a touring jazz pianist and a writer of orchestral scores for symphonic concert halls. In an essay about Vasquez's recent compositions, a student claims that Vasquez blends her two areas of expertise by importing improvisational practices drawn from jazz performance into her work as a composer of fully notated orchestral music.
stem: Which quotation from a music critic best supports the student's claim?

answerOptions.content: “Vasquez first attracted attention as a soloist at after-hours clubs in New Orleans during the 1990s before eventually enrolling in a graduate program in composition at a New England conservatory.”

“In several of her orchestral scores, Vasquez writes out passages that mimic the rhythmic give-and-take of a jazz trio reacting to one another in real time, instructing the strings to copy phrasings she herself improvised at the piano during rehearsal.”
“At her jazz club appearances, Vasquez has increasingly opened her sets with miniature suites whose harmonic structures borrow openly from the orchestral works of Ravel and Stravinsky.”
“The protagonist of Vasquez's recent chamber opera is a frustrated young pianist who longs to leave the conservatory behind and spend her evenings sitting in with a touring jazz quintet.”

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The example of Vasquez writing out, in her orchestral scores, passages that mimic real-time jazz interaction—and basing those notated passages on phrasings she herself improvised at the piano—directly shows her importing improvisational practices from jazz into her work as a composer of notated orchestral music.

Choice A is incorrect because it merely describes the chronology of Vasquez's early career as a jazz soloist and her later move to a composition program; it does not show jazz practices being carried into her orchestral writing. Choice C is incorrect because it shows influence flowing the other way: orchestral works shaping her jazz performances, not jazz practices entering her composed orchestral work. Choice D is incorrect because it describes the storyline of a character in one of Vasquez's works, not how Vasquez herself composes.
